CONTENT.College career: Poor grades limited Jackson's college opportunities, and he originally enrolled at Arizona Western College. He became homesick and dropped out of Arizona Western, moving back to Maryland before he played a game for the Matadors. While living with his parents, Jackson took up jobs at Six Flags America, Chipotle Mexican Grill, and Harris Teeter. An avid NBA 2K player, Jackson contemplated a career in esports.After receiving an email from coach Kale Pick asking if he wanted to play wide receiver for their college football team, Jackson enrolled at Fort Scott Community College in 2019. Although he had been recruited as a wide receiver, Jackson was more interested in playing cornerback, and he convinced the coaching staff to let him change positions partway through the season. In his one season with the Fort Scott Greyhounds, Jackson had 25 tackles and three interceptions, and his junior college success earned attention from NCAA Division I football programs.Jackson transferred to East Mississippi Community College for the 2020 season, but that was canceled due to the COVID-19 pandemic. In 2021, he was given the opportunity to play Division I football as a backup for the Alabama Crimson Tide. Jackson got his first career start in the 2021 National Championship versus Georgia. Jackson finished the season with seven tackles and two pass deflections. On November 21, 2022, Jackson was suspended by head coach Nick Saban for undisclosed reasons. Jackson finished the 2022 season with seven tackles with one going for a loss in nine games before being suspended. After the conclusion of the 2022 season, Jackson announced his decision to enter the NCAA transfer portal to finish his career elsewhere.Jackson ultimately decided to transfer to the Oregon Ducks, with whom he opened the 2023 season as a starting cornerback. During the season, Jackson had three interceptions, seven pass deflections, two quarterback sacks and 34 tackles. For his performance, Jackson was named First Team All Pac-12 for the 2023 season.
CONTENT.External links: Minnesota Vikings bioAlabama Crimson Tide bioOregon Ducks bioKhyree Jackson at Pro-Football-Reference.com
CONTENT.Professional career: Jackson was selected by the Minnesota Vikings as the 108th overall pick in the fourth round of the 2024 NFL draft. Prior to his death, Jackson was in the running to earn a starting cornerback job at the Vikings' training camp set to begin in late July.
CONTENT.Death and memorial service: Jackson, along with two of his former high school teammates, was  killed in a car crash in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, in the early morning hours of July 6, 2024, when another driver attempted to change lanes at a high speed and struck two vehicles, including the one in which Jackson was a passenger. He was 24. Police believe that alcohol was involved.At his memorial service at Dr. Henry A. Wise Jr. High School, two people were shot, one fatally. As of July 14, 2024, local police are investigating the shooting.
CONTENT.Early life and high school: Jackson was born August 11, 1999, in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, to Raymond and Ebbony Jackson. At the age of five, he took up track and field, progressing to flag football two years later. After starting his high school football career at Springbrook High School, Jackson transferred to Dr. Henry A. Wise Jr. High School partway through his sophomore year. In 2018, his senior season at Wise, Jackson made 39 receptions for 612 yards and 12 touchdowns. Wise went 14–0 and captured a state championship, with Jackson playing wide receiver and defensive back.

SUMMARY: Khyree Anthony Jackson (August 11, 1999 – July 6, 2024) was an American professional football player who played as a cornerback. He played college football for the Fort Scott Greyhounds, the Alabama Crimson Tide and the Oregon Ducks. He was selected by the Minnesota Vikings in the fourth round of the 2024 NFL draft, but died two months later in a traffic collision.
